signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Decision + Geopolitical Inflation + Positioning
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7151 testing minor support at 0.7130 ahead of the RBA meeting. Markets fully price a 25bp hike to 4.35% as US-Iran tensions in the Strait of Hormuz drive crude oil +4.39%, fueling global inflation expectations. AUD COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) indicates a crowded bullish consensus; if the RBA fails to signal a peak rate near 4.6% as expected by NAB, a long-unwind risk is elevated. Retail sentiment at 80% short diverges from institutional longs, suggesting retail is fading the trend into the event risk.

signals.title: USD/NZD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: USD/NZD at 0.5862 reflects a 1.026% G8 performance spread as USD outperforms while NZD lags peers. The +150bp Fed-RBNZ carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25%) supports USD dominance despite the RBNZ's hawkish hike bias. NZD COT LF net short -16,833 (23% long) shows speculators are already positioned for weakness, though the G8 laggard status suggests further downside if risk-off sentiment from Middle East tensions persists. Watch for the RBNZ meeting on 27 May to challenge this divergence.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (specs fading the CB) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for 27 May. With NZD long% at an extreme low of 23.0%, a hawkish RBNZ delivery or signal creates significant short-squeeze risk toward 0.6000. Current NZD/USD price at 0.5862 remains pressured by the 150bp carry deficit against the USD, but the positioning extreme suggests the move is overextended relative to the interest rate outlook.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Yield Spread
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1682 vs EuroStoxx 50 correlation at +0.710 (z=+2.01σ) indicates a significant decoupling from historical norms (norm=-0.154). The pair is trading as a pro-cyclical risk asset rather than its typical inverse relationship with European equities. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% creates a +175bp carry advantage for the USD, keeping upside capped at 1.1750. Bear steepener regime in US yields (2s10s +56.6bp) further supports USD funding demand over EUR.
